title: Arrival + Panaji & Fontainhas Walk
excerpt: Settle in and begin with Goa's Latin quarter, riverfront, and easy evening pacing.
content:
Check in around Panaji or Candolim to keep both North and South clusters accessible. Keep Day 1 light and use it for orientation, SIM connectivity, and scooter/car rental planning.

Walk through Fontainhas lanes in the evening, cover old Portuguese houses, and stop at a local bakery for bebinca or poee. Sunset near the Mandovi riverfront is perfect for your first photo set.

Dinner tip: start with Goan fish curry rice or prawn balchao if you prefer spice. Sleep early because Day 2 starts with active coastal movement.

title: North Goa Beaches + Watersports Block
excerpt: Cover Candolim-Calangute-Baga belt smartly without wasting time in repeated traffic loops.
content:
Start early from your stay and do Candolim first before crowd density rises. Keep one slot for parasailing or jet ski in a pre-decided beach pocket to avoid mid-day negotiation stress.

Use lunch around Calangute/Baga but avoid peak hour waiting by booking a table. Post-lunch, shift to Anjuna-Vagator for cliff views and wider sunset frames.

Night option: simple shack dinner and back by 10:30 PM. Goa roads are easier in daylight and your Day 3 is heritage-heavy.

title: Old Goa Churches + Cultural Circuit
excerpt: Move from beaches to heritage with Basilica, Se Cathedral, and curated museum stops.
content:
Dedicate this day to Old Goa and heritage structures. Begin at Basilica of Bom Jesus and Se Cathedral before noon for better light and lower heat.

Add one museum or interpretation center so the architecture has historical context, not just photo stops. Keep your outfit modest and comfortable for walking.

Evening can be reused for a short river cruise or Miramar beach stop. Keep this day slower to recover from back-to-back transit days.

title: South Goa Calm Route: Colva-Benaulim-Palolem
excerpt: Shift to South Goa for cleaner beach rhythm, less crowd pressure, and long golden-hour frames.
content:
Leave for South Goa right after breakfast and avoid carrying too many stops. Pick two major beaches and one viewpoint instead of trying to cover everything.

Palolem and Agonda are better for slow pacing, kayaking, and long shoreline walks. Keep an afternoon cafe break planned to avoid dehydration in open sun.

South Goa evenings are ideal for relaxed dining by the sea. Keep tomorrow as activity + shopping closure day.

title: Adventure Day: Dudhsagar or Spice Plantation
excerpt: Choose between waterfall adventure and curated spice-estate learning experience.
content:
Option A is Dudhsagar route with an early start and expected queue buffers. Option B is a spice plantation circuit if you want a slower educational day with local meal inclusion.

Do not combine both in one day; travel fatigue will reduce your experience quality. Keep one activity block, one food block, and one sunset block.

Use late evening for final shopping in Mapusa/Panaji local market zones.

title: Departure Day + Budget Wrap
excerpt: Close the trip with transfer buffer, budget check, and next-time route optimization.
content:
Keep at least 3-4 hours of departure buffer for airport/rail transfer depending on your stay location. Goa traffic can surprise in holiday windows.

Typical per-person shared budget for this 6-day format: INR 18,000 to 40,000 depending on stay category, watersports, and nightlife choices.

For the next trip, split North and South into separate mini-trips if you prefer slower, non-rushed travel.
